were only handled in EditorState.execute_command_by_id but the app's
execute_command catch-all was calling execute_ex_command instead.

  proper count and keymap, then syncs mode flags
- Added 'command-palette' and 'find-note' as explicit app-level
  commands so they work directly without editor event round-trip

commit.message:
fix: insert mode entry and SelfInsert fallback for unbound keys

Two fixes for the keymap decoupling refactor:

1. ActivateLayer handler now syncs insert_mode/vim_enabled to
   EditorState, sets suppress_next_input, and handles mode-change
   side effects (coalescing breaks, selection collapse, visual start).
   Without this, pressing 'i' changed the mode line but the editor
   didn't know it was in insert mode.

2. Unbound single-char keys in vim normal/visual mode now fall back
   to SelfInsert so the grammar can handle them (e.g. p→paste,
   u→undo). Only applies in non-insert modes to preserve OS text
   input in insert mode.

Added 2 tests for resolve_key behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: decouple KeymapSystem from EditorState

Move KeymapSystem to app level (Memex) so it can serve both editor and
PDF views. Key changes:

Architecture:
- KeymapSystem (app-level): pure key→Action resolution, no buffer state
- VimGrammar (editor-level): operator+motion composition, stays on EditorState
- Central key dispatch in Memex root on_key_down handler

KeymapSystem changes:
- New resolve_key() method: context-free (no content/cursor args)
- New ResolvedKey enum: Action/TransientCapture/Pending/Unhandled
- Count digit accumulation moved from VimGrammar to KeymapSystem

Editor changes:
- EditorState.keymap replaced with EditorState.grammar (VimGrammar)
- Added vim_enabled/insert_mode mirror fields for input handler
- execute_grammar_result/execute_command_by_id take keymap param
- Removed on_key_down from EditorView (keys handled at app level)

PDF layer system:
- Registered 'pdf' layer with 18 keybindings (j/k/g/G/+/-/o/P/w/W/r/R/y/Y/ctrl-d/ctrl-u)
- Added 8 new PDF commands: scroll-down/up, half-page-down/up, zoom-in/out, goto-first/last
- Layer auto-activates on PDF mode, deactivates vim layers
- Removed hardcoded PDF keybindings from app.rs and PdfView

All 131 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add headless client fallback for TUI session reading on remote nodes

When copilot or other TUI apps enter alt-screen mode inside zellij on
remote nodes (e.g. wave2), the shell_wrapper log doesn't capture the
alt-screen content. The read_pane/get_screen APIs return empty content
even though the dashboard WebSocket terminal works fine.

Fix: add _headless_dump_screen_fallback() that attaches a headless
zellij client (ensure_headless_client) and uses dump-screen to capture
the live terminal state. This is the same approach used on lady3jane.

The fallback is triggered in two places:
- read_pane(): when TUI is detected but pyte renders nothing useful
- get_screen(): when read_pane() returns empty content

modernize: Swift 6, @MainActor, Logger, clean platform types

GameLogic.swift:
- Add @MainActor isolation — safe to call directly from @MainActor UI code
- Replace OSLog/os_log with modern Logger API (subsystem + category)
- Remove LogLevel helper enum — Logger has native .debug/.info/.error methods
- Mark cachedWinningPatterns nonisolated(unsafe): written once per key in init,
  never mutated after; safe without a lock
- Add Sendable conformance to Player, GameState, MoveOutcome
- Simplify checkWin for-in with where clause
- Simplify getWinningPatternCoordinates using filter + map + sorted

GameScene.swift:
- Replace os_log + OSLog with Logger
- Remove NSCoder.string(for:) log arguments — use Swift string interpolation
- Remove GameEvent/GameTouchEvent type aliases (confusing indirection);
  use UITouch/UIEvent/NSEvent directly in #if os() blocks
- Use UIColor.systemRed/systemBlue/label/systemBackground/separator
  instead of hardcoded .red/.blue/.black/.white/.lightGray — respects
  dark mode and dynamic type automatically
- Add safe Array subscript extension to avoid unchecked index access
- Simplify resetGame() with array iteration over node names

TicTacToeTests.swift:
- Fix GameLogic.Player -> Player, GameLogic.GameState -> GameState
  (types are top-level, not nested inside GameLogic)
- Add @MainActor to test suite to match GameLogic actor isolation
- Mark static test argument arrays nonisolated(unsafe) to satisfy
  Swift 6 strict concurrency rules for @Test macro access

commit.message:
fix: prevent range formatting data loss + read version from package.json

CRITICAL: handleRangeFormatting per-line diff assumed 1:1 line
correspondence between original and formatted text. When formatting
changed line count (e.g. 1 line → 5 lines), it would replace line 0
with only the first formatted line, silently dropping all other content.

Fix: when formatting changes line count (structural reflow), fall back
to a safe full-document replacement. Per-line filtering is only used
when line counts match (indentation-only changes).

Also: read serverInfo.version from server/package.json at module load
instead of hardcoding '0.7.2'. Removed broken offset-based fallback
that applied original-document offsets to reformatted text.

Google Search Console shows 70 broken URLs from the old Squarespace site
still being crawled. These 404s damage index credibility and waste crawl budget.

Redirect categories (all 301 permanent):
- /hiking/* → / (old personal content, no equivalent)
- /books/* → / (old personal content)
- /interview-questions/* → /interview-prep/
- /study-guides/* → /cert-tracker/ (with specific mappings)
- /bootcamps/* → /cloud-labs/ or /ai-hub/ (topic-matched)
- /guest/* → /cloud-labs/
- /exam-qa/old-slugs → /exam-qa/
- /full-courses/old-slugs → matching /certifications/ page
- /ai-hub/v/* → /ai-hub/ (old video sub-pages)
- /ai-hub/old-categories → /ai-hub/ or /prompt-guide/
- /courses, /full-courses → /certifications/

Uses wildcard routes where possible to catch future variants.
